_comment: Element groups for each exemption under 5 U.S.C. § 552(b).
An exemption applies when all features in ANY ONE of its groups are TRUE.
Each group carries a VERBATIM QUOTE from the statute; a test asserts the
quote appears as a substring of the fetched corpus (after whitespace
normalization). If the quote drifts from the statute, the test fails.
The reviewer's audit is: read each quote and confirm the named features
fairly summarize the elements named in that quote.

groups.quote: (A) specifically authorized under criteria established by an Executive order to be kept secret in the interest of national defense or foreign policy and (B) are in fact properly classified pursuant to such Executive order
related solely to the internal personnel rules and practices of an agency
specifically exempted from disclosure by statute
trade secrets and commercial or financial information obtained from a person and privileged or confidential
commercial or financial information obtained from a person and privileged or confidential
inter- agency or intra- agency memorandums or letters that would not be available by law to a party other than an agency in litigation with the agency
inter- agency or intra- agency memorandums or letters that would not be available by law to a party other than an agency in litigation with the agency
personnel and medical files and similar files the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy
records or information compiled for law enforcement purposes
records or information compiled for law enforcement purposes
records or information compiled for law enforcement purposes
records or information compiled for law enforcement purposes
records or information compiled for law enforcement purposes
contained in or related to examination, operating, or condition reports prepared by, on behalf of, or for the use of an agency responsible for the regulation or supervision of financial institutions
geological and geophysical information and data, including maps, concerning wells

_comment: Statute-grounded 'inverse' patterns. Each entry teaches the pearl that
when named features are TRUE alone, the exemption's co-requirement is
absent and the record is releasable. Each pattern cites a verbatim
quote from the statute showing the co-requirement its absence makes
releasable. The quote-verification test applies here too.

patterns.description: Classified under an Executive Order but the classification is not currently in force
patterns.quote: are in fact properly classified pursuant to such Executive order

patterns.description: Inter/intra-agency memo that is neither pre-decisional/deliberative nor privileged
patterns.quote: inter- agency or intra- agency memorandums or letters that would not be available by law

patterns.description: Personnel/medical/similar file whose disclosure does not constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy
patterns.quote: disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy

patterns.description: Law enforcement record whose production would not cause any of the six (A)-(F) harms
patterns.quote: records or information compiled for law enforcement purposes, but only to the extent that the production of such law enforcement records or information

patterns.cite: Dep't of Interior v. Klamath, 532 U.S. 1 (2001); DOJ FOIA Guide, Exemption 5, p. 5
patterns.description: Klamath: pre-decisional deliberative communications with non-agency parties (tribes) whose own adverse interests prevent them from qualifying as agency or consultant are outside (b)(5).
patterns.quote: the threshold of Exemption 5 did not encompass communications between the Department of the Interior and several Indian tribes
